methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $400M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Space & Aerospace. Range: $274M - $1.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 59.8x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
ClearSpace, a Series A space and aerospace company founded by Luc Piguet, is valued at $400M post-money after raising $36M to date. The valuation assessment draws from a comp universe of 5 public tickers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers including SpaceX, Rocket Lab, and Planet Labs, supplemented by 3 directly comparable private companies at similar or adjacent stages.

Two triangulation methods prove applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A to next-stage advancement, yielding $800M. Peer-median valuation across three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables—Aalyria ($1.3B Series B), Hubble Network ($400M Series B), and Sift ($274M Series B)—produces a median of $400M with a range of $274M to $1.3B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied, as ClearSpace's revenue is not disclosed; the Space & Aerospace public median trades at 59.8x price-to-sales across 6 tickers, but lacks sufficient private data for inference.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $340M to $920M, with a median estimate of $600M. The current $400M valuation aligns with peer-median findings but sits below the stage-progression estimate. Key uncertainties include revenue trajectory, capital efficiency relative to peers, and technology differentiation within a capital-intensive sector where public comps trade at elevated multiples.
